Transcrição Completa: 91 Sales Calls Best Practices 5 25 24
[00:00]
"Okay, welcome to the weekly group call. What we're going to do this week is a little bit of a sales training set of lessons. There were several things that came up this week that I found quite interesting that I wanted to pass down to you guys. Uh we're going to jump right into it."
[00:20]
"Okay. So, to be clear, we've had previous weekly group calls where we have talked in detail about the follow-up process and about the pre-call process. But it has been a very long time since we've talked about the actual calls themselves and some of the best practices that happen on those said calls."
[00:40]
"So lesson number one is a very simple lesson. Put into the chat your answer on this. Is every person who shows up to a sales call supposed to be honest with you? Yes or no?"
[00:52]
"Yeah. Bunch of [ __ ] nos. That's right. Okay. Everybody who shows up to these calls, they are not going to show their hand. They are not going to be transparent with you. They might share some information with you and they might be somewhat transparent with you, but for the most part, people will withhold information, right? That's how it goes."
[01:15]
"So, to be clear, if anybody wants to take a guess, feel free to unmute yourself. How do we how do we get on a sales call withheld information from the person that we are trying to close? What is the easiest way to get information that somebody's withholding out of them?"
[01:32]
"Show them that you're trying to help them solve a problem."
[01:35]
"Sure. That's one way. Anybody else?"
[01:38]
"My answer is questions. Okay. Good quality questions lead to good quality answers from individuals. And it also potentially reveals to us when somebody is withholding something."
[01:52] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Lesson #1: Quality Questions"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Conceito de perguntas de qualidade revelando informações ocultas, comparação entre transparência aparente vs. real
[02:00]
"Have you ever seen one of those videos online where they have a couple and they swap phones and the other person that they're dating gets to look through their phone and you can kind of see when one of them gets a little antsy and they're like, 'Oh [ __ ] like we got to do this now.' Like, 'Let me see that real quick.' you know, and it kind of looks like they're sneak deleting stuff."
[02:25]
"As an example, that's a situation where we can immediately see through observing the person's body language and behavior that there's obviously something [ __ ] up that they're partner is about to find in that device. Okay?"
[02:38]
"Sometimes through questions, we get the answer that we're looking for, which leads to being able to leverage that answer into either additional questions or potentially leading to a higher probability of the deal closing."
[02:52]
"In other instances, it shows us very clearly what the person is uncomfortable talking about and what potentially is the real objection. Okay?"
[03:03]
"Sometimes you get on sales calls, put in the chat, how many of you have got on a sales call and you feel like you just got a bunch of [ __ ] objections from the person that weren't the real thing that was holding them back."
[03:18] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "False Objections vs Real Objections"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Representação de objeções superficiais vs. objeções reais ocultas, linguagem corporal reveladora
[03:25]
"Person gets on the phone, they tell you some [ __ ] that, hey, yeah, like you even ask them like, 'Hey, what's holding you back?' And they give you an answer, but you kind of feel like it's [ __ ]"
[03:38]
"So if we just flat out ask somebody, hey, if you don't mind me asking Nick, like what's holding you back here today? what's what's preventing you from making the decision? And you give me some [ __ ] answer like, 'Yeah, you know what, Jeremy? I mean, I'm pretty sold, man. I don't know. Nothing's really holding me back.'"
[03:58]
"All right. Well, let's get this done today. What do you think? Ah, you know, I don't know. I um I just got to think about it for a few more days. You know, I I'm really close, but I don't know. I'm not quite there yet."
[04:12]
"Uh to be clear, it's very easy for a lot of people to just kind of walk away because they're not comfortable pressing an individual. They view it as hard sales. Okay."
[04:23]
"Real quick, how many of you when you press an individual consider it hard selling and are therefore uncomfortable doing it? Are you uncomfortable with that or are you comfortable with that?"
[04:35]
"For me, I'm very comfortable with it. But I want to be very clear when I say this, okay? To take the advice I'm about to articulate to you, you cannot view this as an uncomfortable like derogatory way of selling."
[04:50] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Hard Selling vs Value-Adding Questions"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Contraste entre pressão de vendas e perguntas que adicionam valor, mindset de vendedor
[04:55]
"Okay, you have to be open to the fact that asking the right questions by no means is hard selling. Asking the right questions is doing what you are there to do."
[05:08]
"To add value to somebody with the product or service that you are attempting to do that you're attempting to sell to them, what has to happen? They have to buy the [ __ ] product or service."
[05:20]
"So without the product or service being purchased, no value is exchanged. Therefore, if you're uncomfortable with asking the right questions that could have led to the sale, what you're really saying is is that you're comfortable not providing value to the other person, which I view as something that's derogatory."
[05:40]
"I'd ra I'd rather add value to the person's life and ask those hard questions than let them walk away with the same exact [ __ ] problem that they have now entering into the call. I'd much rather get the deal closed. Okay."
[05:55] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Value Exchange Philosophy"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Equação: Perguntas Certas → Venda → Troca de Valor, conceito de servir através de fechamento
[06:00]
"So to be clear, very first simple lesson is important to understand. The quality of questions that we ask lead us to good quality answers or potentially observing what the person's actually uncomfortable about."
[06:15]
"I'll give you a perfect example. I'm on a call with a pretty big uh well-known influencer the other day. They have a 20,000 and a $50,000 offer for an info product. They already do a million a month."
[06:30] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Case Study: High-Ticket Influencer Deal"
NÚMEROS: $20,000 offer, $50,000 offer, $1M/month revenue
VISUAL: Perfil de cliente qualificado, estrutura de ofertas high-ticket, volume de receita mensal
[06:35]
"They ask me what I charge. You know, they're really sold on the tornado strategy. They're really sold on working with us. We got intro to the person via a warm referral. Person's saying nothing but great things."
[06:50]
"I remember at one point on the call, this person says, I mean, it's an honor for you to even consider working with us, Jeremy. You know, thank you. Like that type of, you know, respect is is introduced on this call. That's the framing that's held. Okay."
[07:08]
"But to be clear, when it gets time to, hey, what do you charge? Yeah, great question. We charge 15,000 a month for our service fee. So, ongoing monthly fee that you're going to pay, and we charge 10% of a net revenue. It's a rev share that we help generate your business on a month-over-month basis."
[07:30] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Pricing Structure Breakdown"
NÚMEROS: $15,000/month service fee, 10% net revenue share
VISUAL: Estrutura de precificação em dois níveis: fee fixo + revenue share, modelo de incentivo alinhado
[07:35]
"And then naturally, I explain how that works. I say, if you net 150K or less, you just pay the 15K fee. If you net 150k or more, then you pay the difference above 15k for what's owed in the rev share."
[07:52]
"So if you net 200k, you owe us 20k in revshare. You already paid 15k in a fee. You pay the difference of 5k for the rev share in that example totaling 20 for the month. Does that make sense?"
[08:08] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Revenue Share Calculation Example"
NÚMEROS: $150K threshold, $200K net = $20K total ($15K fee + $5K revshare difference)
VISUAL: Cálculo passo a passo da estrutura de pagamento, breakpoint de $150K, exemplo matemático visual
[08:12]
"Other person's like, 'Yeah, you know, that makes perfect sense.' And now to be clear, I can either leave it at that or I can just ask a very comfortable question that is going to lead me to the fact if the person's actually in agreeance with that type of pricing model or not."
[08:30]
"So I ask a very simple question every time that I introduce my pricing. Now I say, 'Hey, just want to just curious what do you think about that? Are you paying rev shares right now? Do you agree with that type of incentive?'"
[08:45] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "The Critical Follow-Up Question"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Script de pergunta pós-precificação para revelar verdadeira reação, técnica de sondagem
[08:50]
"And I get one of the most interesting answers I've gotten in a really long time. I get an answer of I mean, you know, I'm already making a million a month. We pay commissions to salespeople, but you know, they don't have any kind of base fee."
[09:08]
"Um, I don't know. I mean, I'm not opposed to a rev share. What does that tell you right there? Just based on that person's answer, what do you guys think?"
[09:20]
"Somebody unmute yourself and tell me what you think of that answer when you hear it. Does that mean the person's comfortable? Does that mean they're 100% sold on that pricing model? Does that mean that there's likely some reservations?"
[09:35]
"It means that and I've gotten this a lot too is that they think that if they're going to cut a piece of their business or revenue that why am I paying at base or the opposite? Um that's what they're that's what's going on in their head."
[09:50]
"I agree. I agree for sure. Anybody else have any perspective they want to share on that? What's your interpretation of that?"
[10:00]
"They got concerns. are not 100% fully sold on it."
[10:05]
"Yeah, but what part are they sold on the rev? Are they not sold on the revshare? Are they not sold on the fee? Are they not sold on both?"
[10:12]
"They're not sold on the base."
[10:15] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Objection Analysis: Base Fee Resistance"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Diagrama de objeção real identificada: resistência ao fee base vs. aceitação do revshare
[10:20]
"So, to be clear, I countered with, you know, if you did a revshare deal with somebody like me, what do you what would you prefer the deal looks like? Okay, so I want to just take a step back real quick before I continue and how the conversation progressed from there."
[10:38]
"I proposed something that could lead to an objection and rather than just saying it and moving on, I asked a good question right after. This is very important to understand."
[10:50] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Proactive Objection Discovery Technique"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Processo: Propor → Perguntar Imediatamente → Revelar Objeção, técnica preventiva de descoberta
[10:55]
"When I introduce something that could lead to an objection, I just want to ask a question that brings it out. A lot of people don't do that."
[11:05]
"So my simple question in this case was, 'Does that does that make sense to you? Do you agree to that? You know, is there anything have you have you paid a rev share before like that to somebody like me?' Kind of just threw out like a mirage of questions. Try to solicit some type of information from the individual. Okay."
[11:25]
"Now, with that information, I agree with everybody's sentiment here. I don't think that they I don't I don't think that the person wasn't sold, per se, because like I said, factoring in the rest of the call, it's a high probability that deal was getting done."
[11:42]
"However, just the way they worded their answer, it I agree with you guys. It made me feel like maybe they weren't sold on the fee plus the rev share. Maybe they just wanted to do one or the other. And I wanted to flesh that out."
[11:58]
"So, I followed up with, 'Well, how how would the deal look in your eyes?' Like, what what what would we do if you could structure a deal in a way that incentivizes us to want to grow the [ __ ] out of this business together?"
[12:15] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "The Collaborative Reframe Question"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Técnica de inversão: fazer prospect estruturar o deal ideal, abordagem colaborativa
[12:20]
"Now, here's what the person answered with. They said, 'That's a really good question. I always like to delever risk whenever I do a deal with anybody and I agree with the incentives of a revshare. Uh I just want to understand more about the fee.'"
[12:38]
"So then we start to understand well the real objection there is potentially with the monthly ongoing fee."
[12:45] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Real Objection Revealed: Monthly Fee"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Objeção verdadeira finalmente exposta através de camadas de perguntas, processo de escavação
[12:50]
"So I responded with my true answer which is yeah let me let me break it down for you. So to be clear we already have clients that pay us anywhere from like 60 to 100 grand a month."
[13:05] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Social Proof: Enterprise Client Pricing"
NÚMEROS: $60,000-$100,000/month client range
VISUAL: Escala de precificação, anchor pricing com clientes enterprise, validação de valor
[13:10]
"you know, our entry clients, the reason we charge them 15K is because it's enough money that makes me prioritize the deal. And there's a lot of work that needs done at the initial part of us entering into a relationship together as an agency and client."
[13:28]
"And so, simply put, it's a it's a fee that essentially covers our basis to prioritize the relationship right off the bat."
[13:38]
"And if you think about it, after you get to the point where you're netting 150K or more, it's really like you're only paying the rev share."
[13:48] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Fee Justification Framework"
NÚMEROS: $15K entry fee, $150K+ threshold
VISUAL: Justificativa em camadas: priorização + trabalho inicial + transição para revshare puro
[13:52]
"And we have some clients to be fair where they've net 150k or more for so long that we don't even charge them the fee anymore because why would we? The rev share exceeds it by such a significant amount instead of charging their card every month. They just send us a wire for the full revshare amount instead."
[14:15]
"So we're always happy to do a deal like that. But to be transparent, it's just a way to, you know, kind of essentially incentivize our attention on the deal when we very first get started."
[14:28]
"And I always end it with this. I say, 'Does that sound fair?'"
[14:32] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "The Fairness Close"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Técnica de fechamento suave: pergunta de fairness após justificativa completa
[14:35]
"And you know, the person gives me not like a short answer, but still it was an answer that ma made me think like there's more to this. So they said, uh, yeah, no, that that makes a lot of sense. I, um, yeah, I mean, I don't have a problem with that."
[14:52]
"There was a weird pause, okay, in the, yeah, that makes sense and like I don't have a problem with that. Do you think that person had a problem with that based on that type of answer?"
[15:05]
"Drop it in the chat. Did they have a problem with it or do they not have a problem with it? We got a likely yes."
[15:15]
"Once again, put in the chat, do you think that person had a problem with it based on their answer or do you think that they were in full agreement about that answer? Yeah, still has a problem."
[15:28] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Reading Between the Lines: Verbal Cues"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Sinais verbais de hesitação: pausas, qualificadores, linguagem não-comprometida
[15:32]
"Now, to be clear, do I move on or do I ask another good question? For me, the answer is obvious. I'm gonna ask another good question."
[15:42]
"I say, 'Hey, just to be clear.' I'm not really sold that you're sold on that. Uh, just curious, do you have a problem with it? And, you know, can you be honest with me?"
[15:55]
"I want to make sure that I remove any friction that prevents us from doing this deal. I really want to earn your business and I know we can make you a lot of money and I want to make the deal work."
[16:08]
"So, I would appreciate any kind of transparency that you can provide me and let me know if that's going to be a deal breaker or not."
[16:18] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "The Transparency Request Script"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Script completo de solicitação de transparência: remover fricção + ganhar negócio + honestidade
[16:22]
"So, to be clear, that's where I transition from more of like what I consider a general question that helps bring out more information to a little bit of a harder question. Would you guys agree that's a little harder of a question?"
[16:38]
"You know, it's the transparency question. I want to be very clear. I [ __ ] love the transparency question. Can you see the power in that?"
[16:48]
"I love asking a person, listen, I don't want to listen what I say, okay? I don't want to add any friction to getting this deal done. I want to earn your business. I want to be clear. I know I can help you obtain the result that you're after. Okay? And I mean that."
[17:08]
"I want you to be transparent with me and honest. I'm a very direct person. Hit me in the chest with it. What do you actually think about this? Is it going to be a deal breaker or not? be transparent. Let me know. Okay?"
[17:22] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Benefits of Transparency Question"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Benefícios múltiplos: constrói confiança + quebra barreiras + revela verdade real
[17:28]
"When you frame it that way, I want to be clear in the intention of this. Number one, it builds trust. It just breaks down that wall and it helps you get to the real data and the real the real truth of the situation."
[17:45]
"It is completely inappropriate to do if you've misread the situation and the person has actually been being honest with you and you're looking for a deeper answer than you should."
[18:00]
"In this specific case, it was very appropriate based on the answers of the individual. And here's what they came back with. They just simply said, 'Well, what happens if I pay you 15K and you know, we don't make any money? Like, what do we do then?'"
[18:18] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "BINGO: The Real Objection Exposed"
NÚMEROS: $15K investment risk
VISUAL: Objeção verdadeira finalmente revelada: medo de perder $15K sem retorno, preocupação de risco
[18:22]
"So, that bingo is the real [ __ ] objection about the 15K fee. You see, now I can actually work with that and I can overcome that specific issue."
[18:35]
"And I can talk as an example about guarantees. I could talk about that literal situation of what we're going to do in the instance that things don't work and they pay $15,000 and they don't get any kind of result. Plus, they put up ad spent."
[18:52]
"I mean, we're entering into a relationship with an individual and we're asking them to spend upwards of $25 to $50,000 with us in the first 30 days of our relationship."
[19:05] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Total Investment Reality Check"
NÚMEROS: $25,000-$50,000 first 30 days (fee + ad spend)
VISUAL: Investment total real nos primeiros 30 dias, magnitude do compromisso financeiro
[19:08]
"You know, that's a respectable amount of money for anybody to take as a risk whether they're doing a million dollars a month or not. you know, and we as the person closing them on the other side of that deal, we have to make sure we factor that in and are empathetical about it."
[19:28]
"So, to be clear, through that transparency question, I get the real answer. I get the in other words of saying it, well, I'm afraid to give you 15K and not have this work out and you get paid and I didn't get anything."
[19:45]
"You know, that's what they were actually concerned with. And that's why I start that's to be clear, I address it. I start asking different questions. I make sure the objections handled and then we move on to that next step of the process."
[20:02] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Objection Handling Process"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Fluxo: Revelar Objeção Real → Endereçar Especificamente → Perguntas de Confirmação → Próximo Passo
[20:08]
"This coincidentally came up, I think like four different times this week with some of you and different clients where we were listening to sales calls and all of the common objections that would come up, nobody would dig into them."
[20:25]
"You know, a good example, one of the inner circle students this week, his name's Shane, he uh sends me over some feedback. Let me let me help you guys with this."
[20:38]
"So, he says, um, you know, how would you approach this? I'm trying to figure out how this lead can come to this conclusion. He has the income. He had a call with us Wednesday. He scheduled a follow-up call for Friday. There were no financial objections on the call. And then this out of nowhere."
[21:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Case Study: Shane's Blindsided Deal"
NÚMEROS: Wednesday call, Friday follow-up scheduled
VISUAL: Timeline de call qualificada que desmoronou, prospect com income confirmado
[21:05]
"And then here's what the here's what that lead that very qualified lead says to Shane. He says, 'Hey, I'm going to be canceling the call. Um, thank you for your time, but this is not for me.'"
[21:18]
"The reason price all firms have a business model. I respect that, but this doesn't work for me. Best of luck. Love the concept, John."
[21:30]
"Shane's super confused. He doesn't understand. He He feels blindsided by this. Okay. But what did Shane not do? Or in this case, I believe it was his sales team."
[21:45]
"To be clear, what didn't happen on that call? Nobody asked some [ __ ] hard-hitting questions. you know, nobody pulled the objection out of the individual while they were on the call."
[22:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "The Fatal Sales Mistake: Not Digging"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Erro crítico: assumir fechamento sem extrair objeções reais, falha em perguntas profundas
[22:05]
"So therefore, they feel blindsided. They're like, 'Well, this guy's super [ __ ] qualified. We know we can help him.' But at the end of the day, this lead, they did exactly what we talked about at the beginning of this call."
[22:20]
"The lead just wasn't hyper transparent about their current frame on what was being talked about, and the salesperson didn't dig a little bit to pull it out of them."
[22:32]
"So, to be clear, a lot of us sell things that are very high ticket that to be fair aren't probable to close on that call. But that doesn't mean we can't have some hard-hitting questions that pull out if the person's probable to close or not in the coming days or on that next follow-up call that's scheduled. Right?"
[22:55]
"So, I asked Shane a simple question in response. I say I say, 'Shane, do you think that people are supposed to just reveal their [ __ ] hand on every sales call, you know, like you expect a 100% of people to just be honest with you?'"
[23:12]
"You know what I mean? I was like, 'It's one of two things. Either you didn't ask the hard-hitting questions, or two, you guys just simply when you were on that call assumed the guy was going to close.'"
[23:28]
"And once again, that still results in number one, which is you didn't ask the hard-hitting questions to ensure the guy was going to close."
[23:38] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Two Fatal Assumptions in Sales"
NÚMEROS: 100% honesty expectation
VISUAL: Duas falhas: 1) Não fazer perguntas difíceis, 2) Assumir fechamento sem confirmação
[23:42]
"So, I told him to ask a follow-up question. I said in response, I said, 'Can you be more specific? We appreciate your feedback.'"
[23:52]
"Here's what the lead said in response. He goes, 'Hello, Shane. My decision was based on the price. It's too much. I had a ballpark price in my head, but not this. That's all. I think you may have a great system selling the course. Wish you nothing but the best. Regards, John.'"
[24:15]
"So, it's for sure a price objection. What would have been a good question to pull that out of the lead?"
[24:25] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Revealed: Price Objection All Along"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Objeção de preço oculta durante toda a call, expectativa de preço não alinhada
[24:30]
"So, hey Nick, appreciate you jumping on the call, man. U to be clear, this is a $5,000 offer. I just want to make that known. Uh right off the bat, can you just give me some perspective? What have you invested into that's been more than 5K in the recent year? Anything?"
[24:52] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Price Anchoring Question Template"
NÚMEROS: $5,000 offer price
VISUAL: Script de ancoragem de preço: revelar preço + perguntar sobre investimentos similares passados
[24:55]
"You know, why why do I pick that question? Somebody tell me why do I ask?"
[25:02]
"5k is a egregious number from that person's perspective compared to other things they've bought."
[25:08]
"Yeah. So, to be clear, it's like, 'Nick, tell me what you spent more than $5,000 on over this past year, if you don't mind me asking.' Like, is that is that a common number for you or is that pretty high in your lead?"
[25:25]
"I'd say 5K is uh generally, you know, about the number that would be at the top of things I bought over the last year."
[25:35]
"What'd you buy? What'd you buy? It was five grand."
[25:40]
"uh pay four a little over four grand a month on rent. Uh [ __ ] like that. That's probably the biggest one actually. Uh actually some ads. Spent six grand six grand last month on on paid ads. So that'd be comparable a little bit more."
[25:58] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Investment Comparison Discovery"
NÚMEROS: $4,000/month rent, $6,000 ad spend last month, $5,000 top purchase
VISUAL: Mapeamento de investimentos recentes para comparação de valor, anchoring contextual
[26:02]
"So those would be pretty valuable things, right? Like I assume you spend six grand on ads because you're probable to make the money back on it, right?"
[26:12]
"Yeah."
[26:15]
"I assume you spend 4K on rent so you obviously have a place to live. I mean that generates you a great return, right?"
[26:22]
"Yeah."
[26:25]
"The point is, you know, if I showed you today on this call how this 5K offer is such a clear no-brainer return on your investment, you think at the end of the call that we'd have an agreement that we can move forward on this. If I can show you that"
[26:42]
"if it was super clear that I'd get money out of it on the other side, 100% it would be a no-brainer."
[26:48] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "ROI Comparison Framework"
NÚMEROS: $4K rent, $6K ads, $5K offer
VISUAL: Comparação lógica: rent = necessidade, ads = ROI, offer = ROI claro, processo de equiparação
[26:50]
"Yeah. Look, I just got one more thing before we jump into all this, okay? I'm I'm a super direct person, okay? You're not going to hurt my feelings with any kind of feedback that you give me."
[27:05]
"I would truly appreciate if you could be as transparent as you can throughout this process. Let me know if something's holding you back. Let me know if while I'm explaining all this, if something just doesn't make sense or, you know, if you have some kind of disagreement with it, you know, let me know if it's cool and everything, you know, is aligning to exactly what you're looking for. Is that fair?"
[27:30]
"Yep."
[27:32] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Pre-Call Transparency Agreement Script"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Script completo de acordo de transparência no início da call, estabelecimento de comunicação aberta
[27:35]
"I mean, so to be clear, it's like this is what I would literally do at the beginning of the call. This is what I train my sales team to do at the beginning of every call."
[27:48]
"I will say that does open you up because like, you know, if you were selling me a 5K thing, I'm probably thinking to myself, well, I can I could invest that money into ads. I can invest that money into a team member. I could invest that money in S&P and build my net worth a little bit. I could put it into [ __ ] crypto right now, buy some Bitcoin. Those would be my objections, honestly."
[28:15] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Alternative Investment Objections"
NÚMEROS: $5K alternative uses: ads, team member, S&P, crypto/Bitcoin
VISUAL: Lista de objeções de oportunidade: investimentos alternativos competindo pelo mesmo capital
[28:18]
"Yeah, dude. 5K I mean 5K is a respectable number. You know, you got to make sure that you understand although you as a business are commonly selling at these kind of prices."
[28:32]
"Like I'll never I'll whenever I go into a deal with an agency relationship, I always remember I am asking the counterparty to spend upwards of $50,000 in 30 days on me and on ads."
[28:48]
"Think about how many things you could spend $50,000 on. It's like this has got to be a no [ __ ] brainer decision to put 50K into this, right?"
[29:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Respect the Investment Magnitude"
NÚMEROS: $50,000 in 30 days commitment
VISUAL: Perspectiva de respeito ao investimento do cliente, magnitude do compromisso financeiro
[29:05]
"I need the counterparty to be transparent with me in that process. Okay? I've got to I've got to do my due diligence as much as they're doing their due diligence that this is something they're actually comfortable moving forward with."
[29:22]
"When I just go into it thinking it's another sales call and I'm not respecting the other person's money that they are about to spend with us, naturally, I'm not going to be incentivized to ask those hard-hitting questions."
[29:38]
"I'm not going to be incentivized to frame the call at the beginning. I'm not going to be incentivized to pull the information out of you. I'm just going to essentially give you the information like it's a normal [ __ ] everyday sales call."
[29:52]
"I'm going to hang up assuming you're closed just because you're qualified just like Shane did in that example and I'm going to lose a deal."
[30:05] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "The Respect → Questions → Close Chain"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Cadeia causal: Respeitar investimento → Fazer perguntas difíceis → Revelar objeções → Fechar deal
[30:10]
"Can I ask a follow-up question?"
[30:12]
"Of course."
[30:15]
"So, I'm I'm deal I've been dealing with this a lot uh with with u leads as well on the because I'm I'm charging I'm pitching $6,500 base and the the 10% profit share essentially"
[30:32] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Nick's Pricing Structure"
NÚMEROS: $6,500 base + 10% profit share
VISUAL: Estrutura de precificação de Nick: fee base + profit share similar ao modelo discutido
[30:35]
"and even though I don't think 6,500 is that much to be honest like I people are there's different ranges but people I think the biggest issue has been like just the do I want to spend 6,500."
[30:52]
"Some people have been like, I can get someone to run Facebook ads for us for [ __ ] 1,500, two grand a month, that kind of thing. Or just pay a lot of money for them, etc. because they have to spend money on on um you know, ad spend, of course."
[31:12] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Common Price Objection: Cheaper Alternatives"
NÚMEROS: $6,500 vs $1,500-$2,000 cheaper options
VISUAL: Objeção de comparação de preço: serviço premium vs. alternativas baratas
[31:15]
"And so my biggest thought recently is like if that's the if that's repeatedly like the the objection, do I just need to be more clear about like, dude, we just like guarantee results like 100%. like it's like like we're confident in our like we're we're only going to do a deal with with people that we believe that we're going to get results for because we give a guarantee."
[31:42]
"So like great question and this this a really good bridge to point number two okay remember point number one good quality questions uh hard-hitting questions transparency related questions."
[31:58] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Lesson #2: Metaphors and Analogies"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Transição para segunda lição principal: poder de metáforas e analogias em vendas
[32:02]
"Point number two okay is metaphors and analogies. one of the most powerful sales techniques you can possibly deploy on a call. Okay, before I continue to Nick's point, put into the chat, is a guarantee going to solve his problem in that example? Yes or no?"
[32:25]
"Is guar is guaranteeing the results when a client says some [ __ ] to you like, 'Well, Nick, I mean, I don't need to spend $6,500 on you, man. I can just go spend 1,500 bucks some dude on [ __ ] person's concern.'"
[32:42]
"Oh, well, I guarantee the results. I guarantee the results. Potential client. Is that is that going to solve the issue?"
[32:52]
"Not for everyone. No."
[32:55] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Guarantee ≠ Solution to Price Objection"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Garantia sozinha não resolve objeção de preço comparativo, necessidade de abordagem diferente
[33:00]
"Here's what I would do. I would deploy an analogy. Okay? Analogies are one of the most common things you guys will I use them all the [ __ ] time."
[33:12]
"If you guys pay attention to how I talk, I use it in my teaching. I use it in my sales processes. I use it in my day-to-day conversations I have with people to emphasize the points I am attempting to make."
[33:28]
"I use them to frame things in different contexts so people can understand my point from a different angle and perspective. Okay."
[33:38] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Power of Analogies in Communication"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Múltiplos usos: teaching, sales, conversas diárias, reframing de contexto
[33:42]
"So in Nick's situation, let's just understand what he proposed. He's trying to charge 6,500 and the person counters with, 'Well, I can just go spend 2K on that. I don't have to spend 6,500.'"
[33:58]
"Somebody unmute yourself and give me an analogy where somebody has spent less money than what they should have trying to solve a problem. They get [ __ ] hosed doing it and they have to go back and spend more money anyway to solve that same problem. Think of a situation. Think about how many flow through your head immediately."
[34:22]
"Yeah. Buying buying a buying a used car that ends up spent costing way more money because the [ __ ] repairs cost way more than the [ __ ] car."
[34:35] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Analogy Template: Cheap Now = Expensive Later"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Framework de analogia: compra barata inicial → problemas ocultos → custo total maior
[34:38]
"Yeah. And like and like for me it's like I have a personal example of that, right? I could be like, well, you know, I understand your point, Nick, but let let me let me give you a quick example. Okay,"
[34:52]
"I wanted a Rolls-Royce. I really wanted a Rolls-Royce Ghost specifically. And I found that these like 2012 models, I don't know if you've seen them yourself, dude. They're like 100 130 grand maybe. And they're pretty low mileage. I mean, they're like, you know, 30,000 mi cars. I think that's pretty low."
[35:15] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Rolls-Royce Analogy Setup"
NÚMEROS: 2012 model, $100-$130K price, 30,000 miles
VISUAL: Caso pessoal: compra de Rolls-Royce usado aparentemente bom negócio
[35:18]
"And uh I went and bought one. And this thing ended up being a piece of [ __ ] I took it to Rolls-Royce. You want to guess what they quoted me on the maintenance bill for that for that Rolls-Royce that I purchased?"
[35:35]
"$87,000. That is literally three4s the value of the vehicle that I purchased."
[35:45] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "The Hidden Cost Reveal"
NÚMEROS: $87,000 maintenance bill, 3/4 of vehicle value
VISUAL: Revelação dramática: custo de manutenção quase igual ao preço de compra
[35:48]
"And guess what? I was smart. I did a pre-purchase inspection. Okay? I had this company called Lemon Squad go and look at it and tell me it wasn't a piece of [ __ ] They told me it needed tires. That's it. That's all."
[36:08]
"It's all a pre-purchase inspection company that prevents this exact situation happening. That's all they did, you know. And you know what I ended up having to do?"
[36:18]
"I ended up having a [ __ ] ball instead. Okay? I had to drop $98,000 on a 2021 Rolls-Royce Ghost. That was upwards of $300,000 instead of $120,000."
[36:35] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "The Forced Upgrade Cost"
NÚMEROS: $98,000 down payment, 2021 model, $300,000 total vs $120,000 original
VISUAL: Custo final real: upgrade forçado para modelo mais caro, investimento 2.5x maior
[36:40]
"I had to trade in two of my other cars, so I didn't jack up the amount of car payments I was making per month. I ended up only paying about 400 bucks a month in total extra on the Ghost that I I now have."
[36:55] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Financial Restructuring Required"
NÚMEROS: 2 cars traded in, $400/month extra payment
VISUAL: Consequências financeiras: necessidade de trade-in de múltiplos veículos, reestruturação de pagamentos
[37:00]
"It's [ __ ] way sicker. It's cooler. It's newer. And guess what? When I go take it to any kind of Rolls-Royce certified mechanic or a dealer, you know how much they charge me for literally anything since it's under new car warranty? Zero dollars. It's [ __ ] sweet. Okay."
[37:20] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "The Better Outcome Benefits"
NÚMEROS: $0 maintenance costs under warranty
VISUAL: Benefícios do upgrade: melhor produto + sem custos de manutenção + garantia completa
[37:25]
"The equivalent of this in your situation, like look, dude, I know a bunch of people, too, that charge $1,500 to $2,000. I could run an entire business where I charge people like you five grand and I just go pay people like that $1,500 to $2,000 and I don't have to do any work."
[37:45]
"You know why I don't do that? Because they're the shitty Rolls-Royce ghost that has a bunch of problems outside. Just because you can doesn't mean it's a good idea. There's a lot of hidden costs to those things. Okay?"
[38:02] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Analogy Application to Service Pricing"
NÚMEROS: $1,500-$2,000 cheap options vs $5,000 quality service
VISUAL: Conexão direta: Rolls barato = advertiser barato, custos ocultos em ambos
[38:05]
"I had a guy the other day, he's in my inner circle group. His name's Josh. He's working with one of those exact guys. He hired a Facebook advertiser from Pakistan."
[38:18]
"Do you know what the Pakistani advertiser told him? He took a screenshot of the events manager in Facebook and told him that the browser related data and the API related data, all just reporting back to the pixel, was organic sales versus paid sales."
[38:38] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Real Case Study: Cheap Advertiser Disaster"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Caso real de Josh: advertiser barato do Paquistão com incompetência técnica crítica
[38:42]
"as soon as he came into a group of advertisers and literally asked the question, 'Hey guys, my [ __ ] Pakistani cheap as [ __ ] advertiser said that this was organic sales versus paid sales. Is that true?'"
[38:58]
"The advertiser spending a million plus a month on ads laughs and says, 'No, that's that's the [ __ ] pixel data versus the conversion API data that both report back to the pixel anyway.'"
[39:15] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Expert Reality Check"
NÚMEROS: $1M+/month ad spend expert
VISUAL: Contraste de expertise: advertiser de $1M+/mês vs. barato incompetente, erro básico exposto
[39:18]
"You know, it's not organic sales versus paid sales, you know? It's like, yeah, just because you can doesn't mean you should. Does that make sense? You know what I mean?"
[39:30]
"So, it's like, long story short, giving them a a different perspective to think. And by the way, that situation is a real situation."
[39:42]
"So, in terms of a metaphor, Nick, literally Josh in the inner circle, lit a screenshotable example to use that you can go on a Zoom call, share your screen in the inner circle chat, and show that exact example."
[40:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Social Proof Integration"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Uso de exemplo real screenshottable como prova social durante sales call
[40:05]
"No, I mean, I have plenty of example you can use on that. It's like there's a skill difference, right? It's like there's a Michelin restaurant in Chicago that serves hot dogs. Okay. It's a $70 hot dog."
[40:22] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Second Analogy: Michelin Hot Dog"
NÚMEROS: $70 hot dog at Michelin restaurant
VISUAL: Analogia adicional: restaurante Michelin vs. dollar store, mesma categoria mas qualidade extremamente diferente
[40:25]

[40:42]
"And I I hope you're starting to understand my point. We are highly skilled, highly educated. I invest a substantial amount every month into my training and development."
[40:58]
"I have a network of very highlevel advertisers I can access in real time when I have questions, problems. I am one of those highle advertisers myself that gets to share that same set of perspective."
[41:15] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Value Justification Framework"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Pilares de valor: skill alto + educação contínua + network de experts + experiência própria
[41:18]
"At the end of the day, what you're buying when you invest that little extra couple thousand bucks into us instead of one of those [ __ ] Upwork advertisers is skill, education, and expertise."
[41:35]
"You're also saving yourself the additional secondary hidden costs like in the shitty Rolls-Royce example. Okay, we will, believe it or not, you think you're saving money by investing less. You're not. You're actually saving money by investing more. Does that make sense?"
[41:55]
"Yep."
[41:58] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "The Paradox Close: Save Money by Spending More"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Paradoxo de valor: investimento maior = economia real através de evitar custos ocultos
[42:00]
"Now, do you see how well that point really concludes to? Like, think about how your brain's processing what I just said. Everybody sitting here on that call after those two different metaphors and analogies."
[42:15]
"Yeah, I think using a metaphor is important. Metaphor analogy is important versus just making an argument for like, yeah, well, like you can get you can pay $1,500 to get people to like push buttons, but they're not going to tell you the strategies and actually like be incentivized to grow your company with high level strate."
[42:38]
"I think that's less effective than using analogies and metaphors than just like using a straight argument. Um, because it's like direct point that you're making using a story or something that visualizes it in their head. Um, so"
[42:55] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Metaphor vs Direct Argument Effectiveness"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Comparação: Story/Visualização via analogia > Argumento direto, impacto mental maior
[43:00]
"now look, I'm not gonna make some [ __ ] up here and tell you why it's more effective. I have no [ __ ] clue, okay? I haven't studied psychology to figure this out."
[43:12]
"All I know is 100% of the time that I use an analogy or a metaphor in comparison to a direct retort, I get my point across at a much more effective rate than what I would if I just directly object directly addressed it. Okay?"
[43:30] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "100% Effectiveness Rate: Analogies vs Direct"
NÚMEROS: 100% effectiveness rate with analogies
VISUAL: Taxa de sucesso: analogias sempre mais efetivas que respostas diretas baseado em experiência
[43:35]
"It almost feels amateur-ish to directly address something when I could otherwise give a creative metaphor or analogy that provides the same point but from a perspective that I think sells the individual more. Okay. Like I said,"
[43:52]
"I gotta hop on a sales call real quick right now."
[43:55]
"Yeah. All good. Have fun."
[43:58]
"I'll use I'll use it right now."
[44:00]
"All right. Cool. Is anybody else having any questions on the metaphors and analogies for why they're effective in comparison to just directly addressing points?"
[44:15] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Q&A: Metaphors and Analogies"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Abertura para perguntas sobre aplicação prática de metáforas e analogias
[44:20]
"Okay. So to be clear, very important you understand good quality questions that bring out good quality answers that give you just additional data to think with to be able to ask another set of questions or to be able to retort a little more appropriately to what the real situation is."
[44:42]
"Even when somebody comes off like they're sold, asking a good follow-up question about if they're actually [ __ ] sold rather than just concluding to the fact they're sold mentally can reveal to you their level of I'm doing this or I'm not doing this."
[45:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Never Assume: Always Verify"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Princípio: aparência de estar vendido ≠ estar realmente vendido, sempre verificar com perguntas
[45:05]
"There's another simple question very commonly asked. Hey, let me ask you real quick, Rod. On a scale of 1 to 10, one, there's not a [ __ ] chance you're doing this. You think I'm full of [ __ ] 10, you're you're sold. You're pulling the trigger. You want to give me your credit card details right now. Where are you at on the scale of pulling the trigger on this thing?"
[45:30] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "The 1-10 Scale Closing Question"
NÚMEROS: 1-10 scale (1 = no chance, 10 = ready to buy now)
VISUAL: Script de pergunta de escala: ferramenta de avaliação de probabilidade de fechamento
[45:35]
"I mean, great question to ask that can bring out a good quality answer from an individual or just like I said, give you some additional data as talking points to be able to dig a little deeper on the potential issues."
[45:52]
"And like I said, I gave you guys an example of literal sales call I did. The person didn't give me a direct answer. I got the direct answer three answers deep on my questions. Okay, so my point is"
[46:08] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "The Three-Question-Deep Rule"
NÚMEROS: 3 questions deep to get real answer
VISUAL: Camadas de perguntas: superficial → intermediária → verdade real (3 níveis profundos)
[46:12]
"lastly uh and just to reiterate, dude, analogies and metaphors are far more effective than directly addressing the points in most situations just to further sell what you are then going to make a direct point about. Okay,"
[46:30]
"notice how I did that with Nick with his specific issue. I articulated an analogy first. I technically threw a second analogy in there right after that was a little shorter and more brief and then I directly addressed the point."
[46:48] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "The Analogy Sandwich Technique"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Estrutura: Analogia Principal → Analogia Secundária → Ponto Direto, técnica de layering
[46:52]
"It's like supplementing your answer is another way to to phrase that. Okay. Does everything make sense that I've talked about here today?"
[47:02]
"Yeah, very much."
[47:05]
"I just have one question if possible."
[47:08]
"Yeah. Go ahead, please."
[47:12]
"So, you know, I'm just thinking about like when you say, you know, that you're saying the price up front like, you know, before we started the call or like I starting the call, you know, this is this much blah blah."
[47:28]
"How does that compare or like what do you think? I I remember from you know some trainings and to be clear they were not like sales training specifically but part of like on sales if that makes any sense"
[47:45]
"where they say like if you mention the the p the price at the at the front and it's like higher price like high ticket item like people or the pro prospects will only be thinking about the price if they can or can't afford it and everything else you tell them to you know build value or justify it or show them it's worth it kind of goes out the window"
[48:08]
"like what are your thoughts on like you know those two things there's truth on that is there like not really it depends"
[48:18] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Price First vs Price Last Debate"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Debate clássico: revelar preço no início vs. no final, duas escolas de pensamento
[48:22]
"see I've I've been trading on the opposite so way back in the day I was 19 years old I'm working for Grant Cardone his lesson as the time as a as a sales trainer was when you introduce price first everything that you say after justifies why it costs that amount"
[48:45]
"versus if I justify why my offer is valuable and then at the end I introduce the price. It almost leaves the price as a cliffhanger rather than all that stuff that I talked about beforehand could have been otherwise used to further justify why it costs what it cost."
[49:05] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Grant Cardone Price-First Philosophy"
NÚMEROS: Age 19 training period
VISUAL: Filosofia: Preço Primeiro → Tudo depois justifica o valor, vs. Valor Primeiro → Preço como cliffhanger
[49:10]
"So, literally the opposite of what you said. And I want to be clear when I say this. It depends on what it is that you're selling. Okay?"
[49:22]
"Because something that costs, let's say, $15 to $25,000, I mean, why wouldn't you want to disclose that? It's like, Rod, I'll give you a great example. Tell me what a Lamborghini Aventador costs."
[49:38]
"400 grand."
[49:40]
"Why haven't you gone to a Lamborghini dealership and bought one?"
[49:45]
"I don't have 400 grand."
[49:48] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Lamborghini Analogy: Pre-Qualified Buyers"
NÚMEROS: $400,000 Lamborghini Aventador price
VISUAL: Analogia de luxo: todos sabem o preço antes de visitar dealership, auto-qualificação
[49:50]
"That's right. Isn't that a great thing to know rather than wasting your time going to the dealership? You think they want a bunch of people who don't have 400 grand coming onto their lot?"
[50:05]
"Makes sense. Yeah,"
[50:08]
"you go to the [ __ ] dealership to buy an exotic car knowing damn [ __ ] well I'm putting a steep amount like 60 to 100 grand down on this thing or I'm buying it cash. Okay, so only people who show up to a dealership."
[50:25] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Pre-Qualified Traffic Benefits"
NÚMEROS: $60K-$100K down payment expectation
VISUAL: Benefício de preço público: apenas prospects qualificados aparecem, sem perda de tempo
[50:28]
"Think about think about a situation where somebody walks into a Lamborghini dealership and literally is sticker shocked like it costs what? You know, it's like that's just not how it goes."
[50:42]
"You know the price of those cars and then guess what happens? You see all those pieces of content that you get targeted with. You see the [ __ ] videos of the exhaust shooting out flames. You see the videos of people like me [ __ ] ripping around in Ferraris and you're like, 'Damn, that looks fun.'"
[51:00]
"You know, and then eventually, whether you know it or not, you're slowly working up to the point where eventually when you have that amount of cash, you're going to go into that [ __ ] dealership and eventually buy one."
[51:15] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Content Marketing Nurture Cycle"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Ciclo de aquecimento: conhecer preço → consumir conteúdo → desejar → acumular capital → comprar
[51:18]
"It's like literally you're going through that process right now with that type of car, you know? Rob, tell me why you're not talking to me from a mansion right now that costs five mil."
[51:30]
"Same reason because I can't afford it."
[51:35]
"But it's like, long story short, you know what those kind of houses cost."
[51:40]
"Yeah."
[51:42]
"All like the price comes first in those examples and you're just not qualified to buy them. And that's why you're not that's literally why you're not going through the sales processes for those people."
[51:55] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "High-Ticket Reality: Price Filters Prospects"
NÚMEROS: $5M mansion example
VISUAL: Realidade de high-ticket: preço público age como filtro natural de qualificação
[52:00]
"It's like why would I want to replicate the opposite of what makes sense in my sales process? Why would I at a smaller level charging thousands of dollars to tens of thousands of dollars? Why would I replicate the opposite?"
[52:18]
"Makes sense."
[52:20]
"Like it's different if I'm [ __ ] walking into Walmart and I see a katana and I'm like, 'Oh, dude, that's kind of sick.' And then I'm like, 'Oh, it's a hundred bucks, dude. Sold. No brainer. I'll buy that [ __ ] right now.' That's different."
[52:38] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Low-Ticket vs High-Ticket Price Psychology"
NÚMEROS: $100 impulse buy example
VISUAL: Contraste: $100 katana impulso vs. $50K decisão preparada, psicologias completamente diferentes
[52:42]
"That's like an easily obtainable thing. When you get into the thousands of dollars, depending on once again who you're selling to, or you get into the tens of thousands of dollars or even hundreds of thousands of dollars,"
[52:58]
"it's like respectfully, yeah, there's some preparation that usually needs to occur and some mental like comfort, you know, of some, okay, I'm I'm ready to make this decision. You know, you got to respect that process."
[53:15]
"Why put a bunch of people through the sales process and then tell them at the end when that entire time they're probably now going to look at it almost as disrespectful?"
[53:28]
"They're going be like, 'Well, why the [ __ ] didn't you tell me that at the beginning, dude? Like, I'm not qualified to buy this [ __ ] We just wasted each other's time.'"
[53:40]
"Yeah."
[53:42] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Respect Through Transparency"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Respeito mútuo: revelar preço cedo evita desperdício de tempo e ressentimento
[53:45]
"It's it's a respect thing. It's like, 'Hey, Rob, appreciate you jumping on.' Look, I'm not saying you are or aren't qualified for this. I just want to be clear more so it's just out of respect."
[54:00]
"Uh what I'm about to to talk to you about here today, this is a $12,000 investment. And sometimes I talk to people and it's like, yeah, that's [ __ ] nothing, you know, and I'm happy to buy it."
[54:15]
"Sometimes I talk to people and it's like, you know, they got to work their way up to it through time and they know they want it, they know they need it, they know it's going to help, but it's not a right now decision."
[54:30]
"And then sometimes I talk to people and that's such an obscene price. They never spend that kind of money, you know, and they're just not comfortable with that."
[54:42] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Price Disclosure Script Template"
NÚMEROS: $12,000 investment example
VISUAL: Script de três cenários: 1) Easy yes, 2) Future yes, 3) Never yes, framing respeitoso
[54:45]
"So, I don't want to waste your time here today. I I want to be very transparent letting you know. I'm going to show you why it's worth 12 grand. I'm going to build the value in this call here today and demonstrate to you very clearly why it's a no-brainer,"
[55:05]
"especially if you got the money to be able to invest into this thing. This thing is going to help you so much with what you're currently struggling with. Okay?"
[55:18]
"But I just want to be transparent in an effort to save time. Is is that something that if I can demonstrate to you why it makes sense to invest into, you'd consider making this decision at the end of the call here today with me? Or is it something that we shouldn't even talk about and we should just jump off to save time? What do you think?"
[55:42] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Binary Qualification Question"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Pergunta de qualificação: continuar call vs. terminar agora, respeito ao tempo de ambos
[55:45]
"You know what I'm saying? Before we get on to any agency sales call, any agency sales call, 100% of the time, before Leo sets that call on my calendar, he tells those individuals,"
[56:00]
"'Hey, just so you know, we're going to encourage you to spend about 20 to $40,000 on ad spend in the first 30 days. And our service fee is about 15 grand.'"
[56:15] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Pre-Call Price Disclosure Process"
NÚMEROS: $20K-$40K ad spend first 30 days, $15K service fee, 100% of calls
VISUAL: Processo de Leo: divulgação de investimento total antes de agendar, 100% das vezes
[56:18]
"If you're comfortable with that, if we can show you why that makes sense and obviously justify the investment, great. If that's totally out of your range, we can talk to you about some other solutions that we have that might be able to help you"
[56:35]
"where we're just not doing a hands-on deal, maybe consulting or even one of our education products. But can you tell me if that's something that you're comfortable with before I schedule this call out?"
[56:50]
"We do that 100% of the time. We don't even do it on the phone, Ron. We do it in [ __ ] writing."
[57:00]
"Yeah. Just texting."
[57:02] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Written Pre-Qualification Standard"
NÚMEROS: 100% written confirmation
VISUAL: Método: qualificação por escrito (DM/email/texto) antes de agendar, não por telefone
[57:05]
"Yeah. via DMs or [ __ ] email or a text message, you know?"
[57:12]
"Yeah, makes sense. Yeah, that makes way more more sense."
[57:18]
"Yeah. So, anyway, remember like small ticket things, it's like, dude, doesn't matter at all. It's okay. Uh higher ticket things, I am a huge advocate for the opposite of what you currently believe."
[57:35]
"I'm a huge advocate for being very transparent and then building the value after. I think I think that that is just a respectful thing to do. I think you're just wasting your time otherwise."
[57:50] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Small Ticket vs High Ticket Price Strategy"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Regra: Small ticket = preço depois OK, High ticket = preço primeiro OBRIGATÓRIO
[57:55]
"Usually the justification is, well, I'm not going to get as many calls. It's like, yeah, Lamborghini doesn't want hundreds of people walking on their lot that aren't able to buy a [ __ ] Lamborghini. Why would they want that?"
[58:12]
"Yeah, they might only get five to 10 people a day that show up to the lot. But those five to 10 people are very well framed on the costs, what it's going to take to buy it, you know, and yeah, not all of them are going to be ready to pull the trigger,"
[58:30]
"but they walk on the lot knowing the prices. That's the most important part. You know what I mean?"
[58:38]
"Yeah. Yeah, that that makes way more sense."
[58:42] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Quality Over Quantity: Call Volume"
NÚMEROS: 5-10 qualified visitors/day vs hundreds unqualified
VISUAL: Preferência: poucos prospects qualificados > muitos desqualificados, eficiência de tempo
[58:45]
"And I've been trying to stay with that even with my clients, you know, just disqualifying people and even if they get less calls. But I was just curious because I remember, you know, a lot of info perks back in the day. That's what they all say, you know, like, hey, don't talk about the price until the end."
[59:05]
"And I feel like lately it's been the the opposite. So, I was just like curious about, you know, what what's your take on one versus the other and all of that. But yeah, this is super helpful."
[59:18]
"Cool. Does anybody else have any sales related questions? Anything you guys have?"
[59:25]
"Uh, do you have any resources on storytelling to to increase the effectiveness of analogies and comparisons and just uh drilling points further?"
[59:38] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Storytelling Resources Request"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Pergunta sobre recursos para melhorar storytelling e analogias em vendas
[59:42]
"It's a really good book by a guy named Donald Miller called Building a Story Brand. Um, I think that's a very effective like businessoriented book on storytelling."
[59:58]
"Can't think of many others off the top of my head. I'll uh look at my bookshelf, see if I can think of anything when I get back home. And um yeah, but building a story brand is a really good one right off the top of my head for sure. Check that one out and let me know what you think."
[1:00:18]
"Perfect. Thanks, man."
[1:00:20] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Recommended Resource: Building a Story Brand"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Livro recomendado: "Building a Story Brand" por Donald Miller, foco em storytelling para negócios
[1:00:22]
"Okay, other questions. Sweet. All right, good being here with you guys today. Hope you guys enjoyed this and uh yeah, let me know if you have any follow questions in the chat."
[1:00:35]
"Thank you, sir. See you. I guess how grip."
📊 RESUMO EXECUTIVO
NÚMEROS PRINCIPAIS:
$15,000/mês service fee padrão
10% net revenue share
$150K threshold (breakpoint entre fee e revshare)
$200K exemplo de net revenue = $20K total devido
$60K-$100K/mês clientes enterprise existentes
$1M/mês revenue do influencer prospect
$20K e $50K ofertas de info product do influencer
$25K-$50K investimento total primeiros 30 dias (fee + ad spend)
$87,000 custo de manutenção Rolls-Royce usado
$100K-$130K preço Rolls-Royce 2012 usado
30,000 milhas no Rolls usado
$300K preço Rolls-Royce 2021 novo
$98K down payment no Rolls novo
$400/mês pagamento extra após trade-in
2 carros trocados para reestruturar pagamentos
$0 custos de manutenção sob garantia
$6,500 base fee de Nick + 10% profit share
$1,500-$2,000 alternativas baratas de advertisers
$5,000 exemplo de oferta de curso
$4K/mês rent do prospect
$6K gasto em ads mês anterior
$12,000 exemplo de investimento em script
$20K-$40K ad spend encorajado primeiros 30 dias
$400K preço Lamborghini Aventador
$60K-$100K down payment esperado em exotic car
$5M exemplo de mansão
$100 katana impulse buy exemplo
$70 hot dog em restaurante Michelin
100% taxa de efetividade de analogias vs. respostas diretas
100% das calls têm price disclosure antes de agendar
5-10 prospects qualificados/dia preferível vs. centenas desqualificados
Escala 1-10 para medir probabilidade de fechamento
3 perguntas de profundidade para chegar à objeção real
19 anos de idade quando treinou com Grant Cardone
ESTRATÉGIAS E SISTEMAS:
Quality Questions Framework: Perguntas de qualidade revelam informações ocultas e objeções reais
Transparency Request Technique: Solicitar honestidade direta para quebrar barreiras
Three-Question-Deep Rule: Fazer 3 camadas de perguntas para chegar à verdade real
Proactive Objection Discovery: Propor algo potencialmente objetável e imediatamente perguntar reação
1-10 Scale Question: Usar escala numérica para medir probabilidade de fechamento
Metaphor/Analogy Power: Analogias são 100% mais efetivas que argumentos diretos
Analogy Sandwich Technique: Analogia principal → Analogia secundária → Ponto direto
Real Case Study Integration: Usar exemplos reais screenshottable como prova social
Price-First Philosophy: Revelar preço no início para high-ticket, valor depois
Written Pre-Qualification: 100% qualificação por escrito (DM/email/texto) antes de agendar
Binary Qualification Question: Continuar ou terminar call baseado em conforto com investimento
ROI Comparison Framework: Comparar investimento proposto com gastos similares do prospect
Pre-Call Transparency Agreement: Estabelecer acordo de comunicação aberta no início
Reading Verbal Cues: Identificar hesitação através de pausas e linguagem não-comprometida
Collaborative Reframe: Fazer prospect estruturar deal ideal para revelar preferências
ESTRUTURA:
Lição #1: Prospects não são 100% honestos - usar perguntas de qualidade
Lição #2: Metáforas e analogias são superiores a argumentos diretos
Processo de Precificação: Fee base + revenue share com threshold de $150K
Processo de Qualificação: Price disclosure escrita antes de agendar 100% das vezes
Hierarquia de Perguntas: Geral → Mais difícil → Transparência direta
Small vs High Ticket: Estratégias de preço completamente diferentes
Framework de Valor: Skill + Educação + Network + Expertise vs. alternativas baratas
Ciclo de Objeção: Propor → Perguntar → Revelar → Endereçar → Confirmar
Analogias Principais Usadas:
Rolls-Royce usado vs. novo (cheap now = expensive later)
Michelin hot dog vs. dollar store (quality difference)
Josh/Pakistani advertiser (incompetência de opção barata)
Lamborghini dealership (pre-qualified buyers)
$5M mansion (natural price filtering)
DADOS FINANCEIROS:
Estrutura principal: $15K/mês + 10% net revenue
Breakpoint: $150K net = apenas fee, acima disso = revshare prevalece
Clientes enterprise: $60K-$100K/mês
Investimento total primeiros 30 dias: $25K-$50K (fee + ad spend)
Exemplo Rolls-Royce: $130K compra → $87K manutenção → $300K upgrade forçado
Comparação de mercado: $6,500 premium vs. $1,500-$2,000 alternativas baratas
ROI paradox: Gastar mais economiza através de evitar custos ocultos
DURAÇÃO: 1:00:40 FORMATO: Weekly group training call sobre best practices de sales calls
